action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 9 with a larger receptive field (dilation=4) overfit and performed worse on the test set (0.6979) compared to Iteration 8 (0.7175). Analysis: A smaller receptive field (kernel 7, dilation 1) forces the CNN to learn local motifs rather than memorizing the entire mutated region. Furthermore, to extrapolate to >2 mutations, an additive model is theoretically optimal. If we replace the MLP regressor with a simple Linear layer, and use Mean Pooling (which acts like Sum Pooling since lengths are constant), the model will simply sum the contributions of local motifs detected by the CNN. This perfectly models an additive fitness landscape and should drastically improve extrapolation to higher-order mutants. Decision: Revert to kernel 7, dilation 1. Increase CNN channels to 512 for more motif capacity. Replace the MLP regressor with a single Linear layer. Train for 8 epochs.
